license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.73chqiw
title: RNA precipitation
author: Liz Hughes

steps_raw.instruction: Make up the volume of your RNA solution to 500μl with RNase free water.
Add 50 μl of room temperature 3M Sodium Acetate (pH 5.2-5.5)
Add 500 μl of room temperature Propan-2-ol. Mix by inverting a few times. Leave at room temperature for 15-20 mins.
Pellet the precipitated RNA by centrifugation at 12,000 g for 15 mins. Take note of the side of the tube where the pellet will be.

Note the pellet will appear clear and glassy after the supernatant has been removed. Carefully remove the supernatant and wash the pellet with 500μl 70% ice cold ethanol. Be very carefull not to dislodge the pellet. It will appear white and more visable after the ethanol wash. Spin at 12,000g for 2 mins. Repeat this step one more time. (i.e. 2 ethanol washes) Carefully remove all of the ethanol.
Allow the pellet to dry with the tube upside down on tissue at room temperature for 5-30 mins.
Resuspend the pellet in RNase free water in an appropriate volume (usually 30-50μl) Assess the RNA quantity and integrity using the NanoDrop and Fragment Analyzer.
